text.title: Agent Drafts Spec Before Coding Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the spec-first discipline and skip path for AI coding agents on tracker tickets.

rule.statement: On ticket assignment, the agent's FIRST action MUST be posting a structured spec comment (current state / desired state / proposed plan). Code edits before the spec comment are forbidden.
rule.rationale: First-try-pass on coding means the agent skipped discovery and is fabricating intent.

rule.statement: Spec comment MUST contain exactly three sections: `## Current state`, `## Desired state`, `## Proposed plan`. Free-form responses are rejected.
rule.rationale: Fixed shape lets reviewers compare proposals across tickets; free-form decays into prose with no actionable steps.

rule.statement: The agent MUST detect explicit approval before any code edit: Linear thumbs-up reaction OR Jira `/agent approve` slash command OR GitHub label transition to `agent:approved`. Implicit silence is NOT approval.
rule.rationale: Silence-as-consent leads to agents shipping the wrong solution on stale tickets.

rule.statement: Discovery step MUST gather: ticket body, all linked artefacts (Notion spec, Slack thread, prior similar tickets), and code-intel search for the touched modules. Skipping any input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Partial discovery produces specs that miss obvious constraints.

rule.statement: Tickets pre-tagged `agent:auto-approve` (typo, dep bump, log tweak) MAY skip the spec gate. The tag MUST be applied by a human, never by the agent itself.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit bypass label, agents stall on trivial tickets; without restricting who can apply the label, agents self-approve.

rule.statement: The fix PR description MUST link back to the spec-comment URL and the approval-event URL. Reviewers can re-walk discovery without leaving the PR.
rule.rationale: Without the back-link, the spec disappears at merge and lessons are lost.

rule.statement: If ticket is `agent:auto-approve` OR a spike/research ticket OR hot-path incident-response postmortem OR a single-author repo with no second human, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when the gate cost exceeds its return.
